# ferriskey_password_policy (Resource)

Manages a FerrisKey realm's password policy. A realm always has a policy, so deleting this resource stops managing it but does not remove it.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ferriskey_password_policy" "example" {
  realm             = "acme"
  min_length        = 12
  require_uppercase = true
  require_number    = true
  require_special   = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `realm` (String) Realm whose password policy is managed. Also the resource ID. Changing it forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `max_age_days` (Number) Maximum password age in days (null/unset means no expiry).
- `min_length` (Number) Minimum password length.
- `require_lowercase` (Boolean) Require at least one lowercase character.
- `require_number` (Boolean) Require at least one digit.
- `require_special` (Boolean) Require at least one special character.
- `require_uppercase` (Boolean) Require at least one uppercase character.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier (equal to `realm`).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import ferriskey_password_policy.example acme
```
